content:
- Completed the task end-to-end: updated nextest split/config/docs, added the regression guard, fixed the incidental failing etcd_store assertion, and marked the task completed with passes=true.
- Required gates passed: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint. Ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed, and pushed master to origin.
- Post-commit docs publish hook also succeeded and pushed the mdBook sync to the separate docs repository.

- Read progress log and current task registries.
- Confirmed current backlog has only failing non-bug tasks in two stories; done log shows both known bug tasks already pass.
- Next step is to resolve blocked_by relationships from task files/current_tasks before choosing the highest-precedence unblocked task and writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Searched current tasks and task files for blocked_by/meta-task markers; no active blockers or meta-task entries were present.
- Applied precedence: no failing bugs, so the next class is failing non-bug tasks; medium-priority ctl operator experience outranks low-priority HA test hardening.
- Chose the first ctl operator experience task as the most logical next step because the pgtm rename and flattened command tree establish the surface that later config/status/debug/docs tasks build on.

- Read the Ralph progress log and current task pointer.
- Opened the operator CLI rename task; it is still in the initial planning stage with no TO BE VERIFIED / NOW EXECUTE marker yet.
- Next I am mapping the CLI implementation and all references to pgtuskmasterctl/ha so I can write a detailed execution plan into the task file and quit immediately per workflow.

- Searched the repo for operator CLI rename impact. The implementation is currently a tiny clap surface in src/cli/args.rs + src/cli/mod.rs with a dedicated src/bin/pgtuskmasterctl.rs entrypoint.
- Confirmed the main impact areas: parser tests, cli binary integration tests, multi-node harness command expectations, and many docs pages/reference index entries that still name pgtuskmasterctl and `ha ...` commands.
- I am gathering the remaining packaging/harness details now so the task file plan can explicitly sequence code changes, test updates, and docs updates before I mark it TO BE VERIFIED and stop.

- Finished the first-stage task handling for the operator CLI rename story item.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/01-task-rename-the-operator-cli-to-pgtm-and-flatten-the-command-tree.md based on actual repo structure: src/bin/pgtuskmasterctl.rs, src/cli/args.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, tests/cli_binary.rs, tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs, and the mdBook pages.
- The plan explicitly chooses a clean break to `pgtm`, maps `ha state` to `status`, preserves only the currently supported switchover subcommands, records the docs/test blast radius, and ends with TO BE VERIFIED. I am stopping now without verification or execution, per workflow.

- Re-read the carried progress log and current task pointer.
- The active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/01-task-rename-the-operator-cli-to-pgtm-and-flatten-the-command-tree.md.
- Previous Codex pass already wrote a detailed plan and stopped at TO BE VERIFIED; I am checking the task file now to verify and revise that plan rather than starting over.

- Re-opened the active operator CLI rename task and confirmed it was in TO BE VERIFIED state.
- Did a skeptical repo-backed review against src/cli, tests/cli_binary.rs, tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs, Makefile, docs/SUMMARY, and live search results.
- Tightened the plan in the task file before execution: do not hand-edit docs/book, treat direct Cli::try_parse_from callers as first-class blast radius, keep env-var names stable unless a separate decision emerges, add a targeted live-tree search, and require make docs-build before the heavy Rust gates.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and started implementation.

- Executed the CLI rename plan: flattened the clap command tree to root-level `status` plus `switchover`, updated cli::run matching, renamed the binary entrypoint file to src/bin/pgtm.rs, and rewired the binary/in-process tests to use `pgtm`.
- Swept live docs/src references from `pgtuskmasterctl ha ...` to `pgtm ...`, renamed the reference page to docs/src/reference/pgtm-cli.md, and corrected the page synopsis/tree after the bulk rename.
- Targeted live-tree search is clean for old binary/argv references.
- make docs-build passed, make check passed, and make test passed (367/367).
- Next I need to finish the required heavy gates: make test-long and make lint, then update the task checkboxes/passes flag, switch task, commit, and push.
